questions: Should `uploadVideoGeneration` reuse `uploadImageToStorage` (which uploads to the media bucket) even though it's named image-specific? The function is generic internally — it just uploads a File and returns a public URL. Renaming it is out of scope but worth confirming the intent.
The AssetPanel upload button is currently commented out in PropertiesPanel.tsx:117-131. Should we update it anyway for when it's re-enabled, or skip it?
Should audio files also get generation records, or only video? The brief only mentions video.

success_criteria.criterion: Dropping a video file (.mp4, .mov, .webm) onto the timeline creates a row in the generations table with type='video'
The created generation has a valid thumbnail_url (extracted from first frame or fallback to video URL)
The asset registry entry for uploaded videos includes a generationId linking to the new generation
Dropping image files still works exactly as before (no regression)
Dropping audio or other non-image/non-video files still uses the existing uploadAsset path
TypeScript compilation passes with no errors
Thumbnail extraction failure doesn't block the video upload — falls back gracefully
uploadVideoGeneration follows the same pattern as uploadImageGeneration for consistency
No new files created — all changes are edits to existing files
Video generations appear in lightbox and shots features correctly

assumptions: uploadImageToStorage works for any file type (video included) — it's generic despite the name. We'll reuse it rather than creating a duplicate.
Thumbnail extraction failure is non-fatal — we fall back to using the video URL itself as the thumbnail. This handles unsupported codecs gracefully.
The AssetPanel upload path is out of scope since it's commented out. Only the file-drop path needs updating.
Audio files continue using the existing `uploadAsset` path (no generation record). Only images and videos get generation records.
The `createExternalUploadGeneration` function works with `fileType: 'video'` — the generations table type column accepts any string.
No DB migration is needed — the generations table already supports video types (used by AI-generated videos).
